context.description: Implement Redis-persisted rate limiting service for freemium tier enforcement. Free users get 2 PDF uploads/month and 3 dispute letters/month. Must persist across server restarts (not in-memory only). Redis keys prefixed 'ratelimit:' to avoid collision with cache_service keys (prefixed 'cache:').
acceptance_criteria: backend/services/rate_limit_service.py implements: check_upload_limit(user_id) -> {allowed: bool, remaining: int, reset_at: datetime}, check_letter_limit(user_id) -> same, increment_upload(user_id), increment_letter(user_id)
Redis keys use format: 'ratelimit:{user_id}:uploads:{YYYY-MM}' and 'ratelimit:{user_id}:letters:{YYYY-MM}' with TTL=32 days (auto-reset monthly)
Free tier limits: 2 uploads/month, 3 letters/month (configurable via settings)
Premium users (subscription_tier='premium') bypass rate limits entirely
HTTP 429 response with {error: 'rate_limit_exceeded', limit: 2, remaining: 0, reset_at: '2026-04-01'} when limit hit
Rate limit check middleware applied to: POST /api/v1/tradeline-extraction/upload, POST /api/v1/processing/process-pdf, POST /api/v1/disputes/generate-letter
Verify with: redis-cli keys 'ratelimit:*' after upload to confirm keys created
implementation_approach: Step 1: Read backend/services/cache_service.py - understand Redis connection and key pattern
Step 2: Create backend/services/rate_limit_service.py - use same Redis connection from cache_service
Step 3: Prefix all rate limit Redis keys with 'ratelimit:' to avoid cache_service collision
Step 4: Read profiles table subscription_tier to determine if user is premium (bypass limits)
Step 5: Add rate limit dependency to 3 identified endpoints using FastAPI Depends()
Step 6: Return HTTP 429 with informative error body on limit exceeded
pre_analysis: Read backend/services/cache_service.py - Redis connection object and key prefix pattern
Read backend/api/v1/routes/tradeline_extraction.py - upload endpoint for middleware injection point
Read backend/core/security.py - verify_supabase_jwt to understand how to extract user_id
